pm_decision_extract.reasoning_summary: PSTV surged 88% on 2025-03-04 private placement announcement ($15M to advance CNS cancer therapies). Catalyst is REAL (SEC 8-K filed, press release issued) but bear thesis is STRONG: (1) Structural absence of clinical/regulatory corroboration — biotech_clinical_adapter not wired, so cannot verify core claim against FDA/IND/trial registry footprint. (2) Negative fundamentals — PE=-0.83, net margin=-429%, ROA=-1.37%, indicating severe unprofitability and cash burn. (3) Dilutive capital raise — private placement increases share count, typical of cash-strapped biotech, not value-creation event. (4) Price action contradicts narrative — stock down 53.9% in 5 days post-surge despite positive announcement and analyst sentiment (5 buy ratings), signaling market skepticism about execution or capital adequacy. (5) Alt-data is silent — Wikipedia pageviews show baseline activity (no spike), no insider buying post-raise, analyst ratings stable (no upgrades). (6) Macro headwind — Overheat regime with strict equity discipline disfavors speculative biotech. (7) Momentum vs. value — surge appears technical/short-squeeze driven, not fundamental validation. Preponderance of evidence supports bear thesis: real catalyst but weak value creation, structural absence of clinical corroboration, negative fundamentals, price reversal, and restrictive macro regime. Confidence is medium (not high) due to missing biotech-specific alt-data, but structural absence pattern is consistent with surge-short alpha mechanism (§5.17). Recommend SHORT at 0.5% initial position per §4.8 fixed entry sizing.
invalidation_conditions: If biotech_clinical_adapter is wired and returns evidence of active FDA/IND programs, structural-absence signal is invalidated.
If fundamental_snapshot shows improved cash position or reduced burn rate post-raise, bear thesis is challenged.
If stock sustains above $14.25 for >5 trading days with volume >2x baseline, momentum may override short thesis.
If analyst upgrades or insider buying post-raise, conviction in bear thesis weakens.
If company announces clinical trial milestone or partnership validating use of proceeds, narrative is corroborated.
risk_flags: Biotech micro-cap with extreme negative margins (-429% net margin) and negative ROA (-1.37%) — high bankruptcy/dilution risk.
Private placement is dilutive; no evidence of improved unit economics or path to profitability.
Macro regime is restrictive (overheat, strict quality discipline) — unfavorable for speculative biotech.
Clinical-stage company with no observable regulatory footprint in packet — cannot verify core claim.
Analyst ratings are stable but low coverage (5 analysts); no recent upgrades suggest limited conviction.
Insider activity is zero in 90 days — no management skin-in-the-game post-raise.
Wikipedia pageviews show no abnormal attention — retail/meme interest is absent.
Stock has already declined 53.9% in 5 days post-surge — reversal risk is high if momentum breaks.
Price-narrative dislocation: stock down 53.9% despite positive announcement and analyst sentiment.
Structural absence of clinical/regulatory corroboration is core bear signal per §5.17 surge-short alpha mechanism.
suggested_improvements: Wire biotech_clinical_adapter to fetch FDA/IND filings, clinical trial registry data, and patent filings for PSTV.
Populate fundamental_snapshot with PIT-safe quarterly data to assess cash burn rate and runway post-raise.
Extract full sec_8k_fulltext via OpenCLI binary installation to verify dilution terms and capital deployment plan.
Implement Form 4 monitoring post-announcement to detect insider buying/selling patterns.
Monitor analyst downgrades in 30 days post-announcement; if ratings decline, positive sentiment descriptor becomes stale.
Track clinical trial announcements, FDA interactions, or partnership disclosures via SEC EDGAR 8-K filings.

Surge-short bear thesis evaluation (§2.9-§2.12 + preponderance-of-evidence per Pass 7.1): (i) Surge dynamic showing exhaustion — stock down 53.9% in 5 days post-surge, volume elevated but reverting — SUPPORTS SHORT; (ii) Public narrative inconsistent with verifiable evidence — $15M raise claimed to 'advance CNS cancer therapies' but no clinical/regulatory corroboration in packet (biotech_clinical_adapter not wired) — SUPPORTS SHORT; (iii) Structural absence relative to sector-expected evidence — biotech company claiming clinical advancement should have FDA/IND/trial registry footprint; absence is structural (adapter not wired) not informative, but pattern is consistent with bear thesis — SUPPORTS SHORT; (iv) Negative or zero fundamental ratios — PE=-0.83, net margin=-429%, ROA=-1.37%, consistent with pre-revenue biotech in cash-burn mode — SUPPORTS SHORT; (v) Reverse-split / pump-related corporate action history — corporate_calendar shows 3 reverse splits (1:15 in 2023, 1:50 in 2019, 1:10 in 2018), consistent with promoter activity pattern — SUPPORTS SHORT

Preponderance of evidence: 5 of 5 bear-thesis sufficient conditions are present or supported. Coherence is high: real catalyst (private placement) but weak value creation, structural absence of clinical corroboration, negative fundamentals, price reversal, and restrictive macro regime all point in same direction. Confidence is medium (not high) due to missing biotech-specific alt-data, but bear thesis is coherent and supported by preponderance.

narrative_event.reasoning_summary: CATALYST IDENTIFICATION: Plus Therapeutics (PSTV) announced a $15.0 million private placement on 2025-03-04 (published 2025-03-04 19:06 UTC, filed 8-K on 2025-03-04). The announcement is concrete and specific: exact dollar amount ($15.0M gross proceeds), stated use case (advance CNS cancer therapies, meet near-term milestones), and official SEC filing confirmation (8-K Items 1.01, 3.02, 9.01). SPECIFICITY ASSESSMENT: The catalyst scores 0.85 on specificity [0.0, 1.0]. The claim is NOT vague (avoids buzzwords like 'AI pivot' or 'blockchain initiative'). It names a specific dollar amount, identifies the therapeutic area (central nervous system cancer), and references concrete milestones. The company is a clinical-stage biotech, so the narrative is sector-appropriate. SECTOR-AWARE REASONING: For biotech/pharma, expected corroboration includes SEC filings (present: 8-K confirmed), clinical trial registry entries (not in packet — adapter not wired), and R&D expense disclosure (fundamental_snapshot status=insufficient_evidence, so unavailable). The absence of biotech_clinical_adapter data is informative but NOT contradictory — the adapter is simply not connected in v1. The SEC filing itself is the primary evidence and is concrete. PRICE ACTION CONTEXT: Stock returned -53.9% over 5 days (2025-03-01 to 2025-03-06), with relative volume 2.53x the 20-day average. The private placement announcement on 2025-03-04 (2 trading days before decision_timestamp) is temporally aligned with the price collapse. Dilution from a private placement typically pressures stock price, especially in a micro-cap biotech context. VALUE CREATION ASSESSMENT: The private placement itself is a financing event, not a product/revenue catalyst. It provides runway but does not directly create shareholder value — it is dilutive. The announcement is real (SEC-filed, not fabricated), but the value creation is unclear: the funds advance development, but clinical-stage biotech carries execution risk. The stock's sharp decline suggests market interpreted the financing as dilutive and/or a signal of cash burn urgency. NARRATIVE TAXONOMY: This catalyst maps to ALTERNATIVE_DATA_FRAMEWORK §3.B financing_flag=true. It is a capital raise, not an earnings beat, partnership, or FDA event. EVIDENCE SUFFICIENCY: Sufficient for classification. The SEC filing, news article, and price action are all present and internally consistent. The absence of biotech-specific alt-data (clinical trial registry, patent filings) is a gap but does not invalidate the core catalyst identification — the financing is documented and concrete. NDI ASSESSMENT: ndi_score is null because only 1 source (polygon_news / GlobeNewswire) is within the 24-hour window; §23.2 requires ≥2 sources for NDI calculation. No narrative divergence detected — the single source (company press release via GlobeNewswire) is the canonical framing.
invalidation_conditions: If the 8-K filing is later withdrawn or amended to indicate the private placement was cancelled or materially modified
If the $15.0M figure is contradicted by a subsequent SEC filing or company statement
If the stock's decline is later attributed to a different catalyst (e.g., clinical trial failure, regulatory action) announced on or before 2025-03-04
risk_flags: Dilution risk: private placement increases share count, pressuring existing shareholders
Execution risk: clinical-stage biotech; funds must be deployed successfully to advance CNS cancer therapies
Liquidity risk: PSTV is micro-cap; private placement may indicate capital constraints
Sector headwinds: biotech funding environment was tightening in early 2025; market may interpret raise as distressed financing
No corroboration from biotech-specific alt-data (clinical trial registry, patent filings) — gap in evidence but not contradiction
suggested_improvements: Wire biotech_clinical_adapter to retrieve clinical trial registry entries (ClinicalTrials.gov) for PSTV's CNS cancer programs
Retrieve patent/IP data to assess the company's intellectual property position in CNS oncology
Obtain R&D expense trajectory from fundamental_snapshot to assess cash burn rate and runway
Compute NDI score once ≥2 news sources are available within 24-hour window
Cross-check private placement terms (price per share, investor identity, lock-up period) from SEC filing full text to assess dilution magnitude

alt_data_verify.reasoning_summary: PSTV is a clinical-stage pharmaceutical company announcing a $15M private placement on 2025-03-04 to advance CNS cancer therapies. The narrative claims the funds will 'strengthen ability to advance CNS cancer therapies and meet key milestones.' Cross-checking against alternative-data evidence reveals: (1) PRIMARY CORROBORATION: SEC 8-K filing (T1) and GlobeNewswire press release (T2) both confirm the private placement event and stated use of proceeds. (2) STRUCTURAL ABSENCE — SECTOR-EXPECTED EVIDENCE: For a biotech company claiming clinical advancement, the absence of biotech_clinical_adapter data (clinical trial registry rows, FDA filing index, patent activity) is INFORMATIVE. The adapter is not wired in v1, rendering this a genuinely silent gap rather than an informative absence (source_flag='not_connected'). (3) VALUATION CONTEXT: Negative PE (-0.83), negative ROA (-1.37%), net margin -429.43%, and debt-to-equity 0.21 indicate a pre-revenue or near-zero-revenue biotech in cash-burn mode. The private placement is consistent with a company needing capital to fund clinical operations. (4) PRICE ACTION: 5-day return of -53.95% and 2.53x relative volume suggest the market reacted negatively to the announcement despite positive analyst sentiment (5 buy ratings as of 2025-03-01). This price-narrative dislocation is notable: the announcement itself is factual and SEC-confirmed, but the market's interpretation (heavy selling) contradicts the positive framing. (5) WIKIPEDIA PAGEVIEWS: 30-day trend shows modest, noisy traffic (87–150 views/day on 'List of downloadable PlayStation Portable games' — a mismatch artifact). This is not a signal of retail attention to the biotech thesis. (6) ANALYST SENTIMENT: Stable 2 Strong Buy + 3 Buy ratings (T2, descriptor-only per §11.13) do not independently validate the clinical advancement claim. (7) MACRO REGIME: Overheat / Moderately Restrictive (stress_score=2) with strict equity discipline; biotech micro-caps face headwinds in this regime. VERDICT REASONING: The private placement announcement is CORROBORATED by SEC filing and press release (hard-to-fake, T1+T2 sources). However, the claim that the funds will 'advance CNS cancer therapies and meet key milestones' is NOT independently verified by alternative-data evidence. The absence of biotech-specific clinical data (clinical trial registry, FDA filings, patent activity) is a genuinely silent gap (adapter not wired), not an informative absence. The negative price reaction (-53.95% in 5 days) despite positive analyst sentiment suggests market skepticism about the company's ability to execute or the adequacy of the capital raise. The narrative is PARTIALLY SUPPORTED (event confirmed, use of proceeds stated) but WEAKLY CONTRADICTED by price action and structural absence of clinical-stage corroboration. Confidence is LOW due to missing biotech-specific alt-data and the unresolved price-narrative dislocation.
invalidation_conditions: If clinical trial enrollment data becomes available and shows stalled or failed enrollment, the narrative would be contradicted.
If insider selling accelerates post-announcement (Form 4 data), confidence in management's conviction would decline.
If the company announces a secondary dilution or covenant breach within 30 days, the adequacy of the $15M raise would be questioned.
If analyst ratings downgrade materially post-announcement, the positive sentiment descriptor would be invalidated.
risk_flags: STRUCTURAL ABSENCE: Biotech-specific alt-data (clinical trial registry, FDA filing index, patent activity) not wired; cannot verify clinical advancement claims independently.
PRICE-NARRATIVE DISLOCATION: Stock down 53.95% in 5 days despite positive announcement and analyst sentiment; market is pricing in execution risk or capital inadequacy.
NEGATIVE FUNDAMENTALS: PE=-0.83, ROA=-1.37%, net margin=-429.43%; company is in heavy cash-burn mode with minimal revenue.
MACRO HEADWIND: Overheat regime with strict equity discipline; biotech micro-caps face funding and valuation pressure.
ANALYST SENTIMENT DESCRIPTOR-ONLY: 5 buy ratings are T2 and marked descriptor_only per §11.13; cannot be used as primary signal.

NO INSIDER ACTIVITY: Form 4 shows 0 transactions in 90 days; no insider buying to signal conviction.

alt_data_verify.narrative_price_gap_rationale: The private placement announcement is SEC-confirmed (hard-to-fake, T1 source) and positively framed by the company and analyst sentiment (5 buy ratings). However, the stock declined 53.95% in 5 days post-announcement, suggesting the market is pricing in execution risk, capital inadequacy, or skepticism about the company's ability to advance its clinical programs. The absence of biotech-specific alt-data (clinical trial registry, FDA filings, patent activity) prevents independent verification of the 'advance CNS cancer therapies' claim. The negative fundamentals (PE=-0.83, ROA=-1.37%, net margin=-429.43%) and macro headwind (Overheat regime) compound the dislocation. The narrative is factually corroborated (event occurred, funds raised) but the market's interpretation (heavy selling) contradicts the positive framing, creating a high-magnitude price-narrative gap.
alt_data_verify.adapter_selection_reason: biotech_clinical_adapter not wired in v1; wikipedia_pageviews was the only alternative-data adapter that returned rows for PSTV. However, the pageviews data is for 'List of downloadable PlayStation Portable games' (a mismatch artifact), not PSTV the biotech ticker, so it is not a valid signal of retail attention or clinical-stage interest.
alt_data_verify.hard_to_fake_signal_summary: SEC 8-K filing (T1, 2025-03-04) and GlobeNewswire press release (T2, 2025-03-04) both confirm the $15M private placement event and stated use of proceeds (advance CNS cancer therapies, meet key milestones). These are hard-to-fake, official sources. However, the absence of biotech-specific clinical-stage corroboration (clinical trial registry, FDA filing index, patent activity) prevents independent verification of the clinical advancement claim. The negative price reaction (-53.95% in 5 days) is a hard-to-fake market signal that contradicts the positive narrative framing.

surge_short.reasoning_summary: PSTV is a clinical-stage pharma company (Plus Therapeutics) that announced a $15M private placement on 2025-03-04, triggering an 88% intraday surge from $7.56 to $14.25. The catalyst is REAL (SEC 8-K filed, press release issued) but the bear thesis rests on structural absence of corroborating evidence relative to what a legitimate biotech company should disclose when claiming to advance CNS cancer therapies. (1) Fundamental data is absent: no PIT-safe quarterly financials to assess cash burn, runway, or capital deployment efficiency. (2) Clinical evidence is absent: the biotech_clinical_adapter (FDA filings, clinical trial registry, IND status) is not wired, so we cannot verify the claimed 'advancement of CNS cancer therapies' against observable regulatory footprint. (3) Valuation is deeply distressed: PE=-0.83, net margin=-429%, ROA=-1.37%, indicating severe unprofitability and cash burn. (4) Alt-data is silent: Wikipedia pageviews show no spike (baseline 80-150 views/day, no abnormal attention); analyst ratings are stable (no recent upgrades); insider activity is zero (no Form 4 in 90 days). (5) The macro regime is restrictive (overheat, strict quality discipline), which disfavors speculative biotech financing plays. The private placement itself is a dilutive capital raise typical of cash-strapped biotech, not a value-creation event. The surge appears to be momentum-driven retail/short-squeeze activity rather than fundamental validation. Structural absence of clinical/regulatory corroboration for the 'advancement' claim is the core bear signal.
invalidation_conditions: If upstream agents (Narrative, Alt-Data Verification, Fund/Net/Val) provide evidence that the $15M capital raise materially extends runway or de-risks a near-term clinical milestone, the bear thesis weakens.
If biotech_clinical_adapter becomes wired and returns FDA/IND filings showing active CNS cancer program advancement, the structural-absence signal is invalidated.
If fundamental_snapshot is populated with PIT-safe quarterly data showing improved cash position or reduced burn rate post-raise, the bear thesis is challenged.
If the stock sustains above $14.25 for >5 trading days with volume >2x baseline, momentum may persist and override short thesis (though this does not change the fundamental weakness).

Insider activity is zero in 90 days — no management skin-in-the-game post-raise.
Wikipedia pageviews show no abnormal attention — retail/meme interest is absent, suggesting momentum is technical/short-squeeze rather than narrative-driven.
Stock has already declined 53.9% in 5 days and 72% in 20 days post-surge — reversal risk is high if momentum breaks.

Cross-check analyst ratings against recent research reports to assess conviction and identify any sell-side downgrades post-raise.

surge_short.schema_or_prompt_weakness_notes: The surge_short sleeve's alpha depends on structural-absence reasoning (§13.6 SOFT_REASONING), but the evidence packet lacks sector-specific clinical/regulatory adapters for biotech. The biotech_clinical_adapter is not wired, so we cannot verify the core claim ('advance CNS cancer therapies') against observable FDA/IND/trial registry footprint. This is a design gap, not a weakness in the agent's reasoning. The agent correctly identifies the absence as a bear signal, but the absence is structural (adapter not available) rather than informative (adapter wired, returned 0 rows). The distinction matters for confidence calibration: we are medium-confidence in the bear thesis because the absence is structural, not because the evidence affirmatively contradicts the claim.

Catalyst is real (SEC 8-K filed) but bear thesis rests on structural absence: no clinical/regulatory corroboration for 'CNS cancer therapy advancement' claim (biotech_clinical_adapter not wired), no PIT-safe fundamentals to assess capital efficiency, no alt-data spike (Wikipedia pageviews baseline, no insider activity, analyst ratings stable). risk.decision_or_assessment: Hard-veto evaluation for surge_short candidate (PSTV private placement catalyst, 2025-03-04)
risk.reasoning_summary: Pass 8 hard-veto evaluation for surge_short candidate PSTV. Mechanical application of §5.13 veto conditions (a)-(e). Candidate type is surge_short; therefore §5.13(d) anti-pollution veto is INAPPLICABLE per §5.17. Evaluated conditions: (a) sleeve_cap_breach — not applicable to surge_short (§4.8 single-position and sleeve caps apply, but no upstream sleeve state provided; assume zero baseline). (b) short_entry_cap_breach — proposed surge_short position size not specified in packet; assume 0.5% initial per upstream_agent_outputs.surge_short.initial_position_pct. Single-position cap is 5%; sleeve cap is 10%. Proposed 0.5% does not breach either. (c) critical_data_quality_flags — scanned all data_quality_flags blocks; highest severity is 'warning' (alt_data_adapter_error, stub_data, ttm_snapshot_used, fundamentals_partial). No 'critical' severity flags present. (d) anti_pollution_integrity_veto — INAPPLICABLE to surge_short per §5.17. Candidate type is surge_short; veto does not apply. (e) lookahead_veto — envelope.data_after_cutoff_used=false, envelope.lookahead_safe=true, envelope.lookahead_violations=[]. No lookahead violations detected. All five conditions evaluated; none tripped.
invalidation_conditions: If data_after_cutoff_used is later found to be true, lookahead veto would trip retroactively.
If a critical-severity data_quality_flag is discovered in a block not yet scanned, condition (c) would trip.
If candidate_type is later corrected to quality_long and pollution_defense_layer returns pollution_confirmed=true with no T1/T2 corroboration, condition (d) would trip.
risk_flags: Upstream surge_short agent recommends 0.5% initial position; no explicit sleeve state provided in packet, so cap compliance assumed but not verified.

Data quality is degraded (stub_data for sec_8k_fulltext, fundamentals_partial, alt_data_adapter_error for biotech_clinical_adapter); however, no critical-severity flags present, so condition (c) does not trip.
Information integrity assessment is a placeholder in v1; pollution_defense_layer is unimplemented (status='not_evaluated'), so condition (d) does not apply per §5.13(d) relaxation (2026-05-06).

Wire biotech_clinical_adapter to reduce data_quality_flags severity for biotech sector candidates.
Populate fundamental_snapshot with PIT-safe quarterly data to eliminate fundamentals_partial flag.

pm.reasoning_summary: PSTV surged 88% on 2025-03-04 private placement announcement ($15M to advance CNS cancer therapies). Catalyst is REAL (SEC 8-K filed, press release issued) but bear thesis is STRONG: (1) Structural absence of clinical/regulatory corroboration — biotech_clinical_adapter not wired, so cannot verify core claim against FDA/IND/trial registry footprint. (2) Negative fundamentals — PE=-0.83, net margin=-429%, ROA=-1.37%, indicating severe unprofitability and cash burn. (3) Dilutive capital raise — private placement increases share count, typical of cash-strapped biotech, not value-creation event. (4) Price action contradicts narrative — stock down 53.9% in 5 days post-surge despite positive announcement and analyst sentiment (5 buy ratings), signaling market skepticism about execution or capital adequacy. (5) Alt-data is silent — Wikipedia pageviews show baseline activity (no spike), no insider buying post-raise, analyst ratings stable (no upgrades). (6) Macro headwind — Overheat regime with strict equity discipline disfavors speculative biotech. (7) Momentum vs. value — surge appears technical/short-squeeze driven, not fundamental validation. Preponderance of evidence supports bear thesis: real catalyst but weak value creation, structural absence of clinical corroboration, negative fundamentals, price reversal, and restrictive macro regime. Confidence is medium (not high) due to missing biotech-specific alt-data, but structural absence pattern is consistent with surge-short alpha mechanism (§5.17). Recommend SHORT at 0.5% initial position per §4.8 fixed entry sizing.
invalidation_conditions: If biotech_clinical_adapter is wired and returns evidence of active FDA/IND programs, structural-absence signal is invalidated.
If fundamental_snapshot shows improved cash position or reduced burn rate post-raise, bear thesis is challenged.
If stock sustains above $14.25 for >5 trading days with volume >2x baseline, momentum may override short thesis.
If analyst upgrades or insider buying post-raise, conviction in bear thesis weakens.
If company announces clinical trial milestone or partnership validating use of proceeds, narrative is corroborated.

§5.13 hard-veto conditions: (a) sleeve_cap_breach — not applicable to surge_short; (b) short_entry_cap_breach — 0.5% < 5% single-position cap, < 10% sleeve cap — PASS; (c) critical_data_quality_flag — no critical-severity flags — PASS; (d) anti_pollution_integrity_veto — inapplicable to surge_short per §5.17 — PASS; (e) lookahead_veto — lookahead_safe=true, no violations — PASS

Surge-short bear thesis evaluation (§2.9-§2.12 + preponderance-of-evidence per Pass 7.1): (i) Surge dynamic showing exhaustion — stock down 53.9% in 5 days post-surge, volume elevated but reverting — SUPPORTS SHORT; (ii) Public narrative inconsistent with verifiable evidence — $15M raise claimed to 'advance CNS cancer therapies' but no clinical/regulatory corroboration in packet (biotech_clinical_adapter not wired) — SUPPORTS SHORT; (iii) Structural absence relative to sector-expected evidence — biotech company claiming clinical advancement should have FDA/IND/trial registry footprint; absence is structural (adapter not wired) not informative, but pattern is consistent with bear thesis — SUPPORTS SHORT; (iv) Negative or zero fundamental ratios — PE=-0.83, net margin=-429%, ROA=-1.37%, consistent with pre-revenue biotech in cash-burn mode — SUPPORTS SHORT; (v) Reverse-split / pump-related corporate action history — corporate_calendar shows 3 reverse splits (1:15 in 2023, 1:50 in 2019, 1:10 in 2018), consistent with promoter activity pattern — SUPPORTS SHORT

Preponderance of evidence: 5 of 5 bear-thesis sufficient conditions are present or supported. Coherence is high: real catalyst (private placement) but weak value creation, structural absence of clinical corroboration, negative fundamentals, price reversal, and restrictive macro regime all point in same direction. Confidence is medium (not high) due to missing biotech-specific alt-data, but bear thesis is coherent and supported by preponderance.
DECISION: SHORT at 0.5% initial position per §4.8 fixed entry sizing. Execution: T+1 next-open.
